compiled_state.title: Write RED test for check-plugin-boundary pre-commit hook
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Story: c73a-1918
testing_mode: RED
Layer: 0 (test infrastructure)

Create tests/hooks/test-check-plugin-boundary.sh with executable bit set.

The test must be RED before Task 2 (hook does not exist yet).

Assertions (mapped to done definitions):
1. ASSERT: plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-boundary.sh does NOT yet exist → test will FAIL once hook is created (RED gate)
2. ASSERT: After hook is created (post-T2), hook is executable
3. ASSERT: plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf exists
4. ASSERT: conf contains a comment block explaining how to add new paths
5. ASSERT: running hook against post-S1 plugins/dso/ directory (as staged files) exits 0 (zero violations)
6. ASSERT: running hook with a staged plugins/dso/docs/designs/test.md exits non-zero AND output contains the allowlist filename
7. ASSERT: running hook when allowlist file is missing exits 0 (fail-open)
8. ASSERT: .pre-commit-config.yaml contains entry referencing check-plugin-boundary.sh

Structure:
- Use PASS/FAIL counters, exit 1 if any FAIL
- Use temp git repo for staged-file assertions (mock git diff --cached output via stdin or env override)
- Set executable bit on test file: chmod +x

Files:
- tests/hooks/test-check-plugin-boundary.sh (create, chmod +x)

Constraints:
- No mocking of the allowlist conf itself — create a temp one in the test
- Do not hit the actual pre-commit framework — test the hook script directly
- The test for assertion 1 (hook non-existence) is the RED gate — remove this assertion in T2 when the hook is created and the test must go GREEN
